  • Examples

Cycle Counters (arch/cycle.h)
[Tile Architecture Headers]

Detailed Description

Interface to the "cycle count", which is a 64-bit number, initialized to zero at boot, and incremented at the end of each machine cycle.

Functions

uint_reg_t get_cycle_count_low (void)
 Get the low bits of the current cycle count.
uint_reg_t get_cycle_count_high (void)
 Get the high 32 bits of the current cycle count (not defined for TILE-Gx).
unsigned long long get_cycle_count (void)
 Get the current cycle count.
void cycle_relax (void)
 Delay for a brief period.

Function Documentation

uint_reg_t get_cycle_count_low ( void   ) 

Get the low bits of the current cycle count.

This function returns only as many bits as will fit in one machine register. On TILE-Gx all the bits are returned.

uint_reg_t get_cycle_count_high ( void   ) 

Get the high 32 bits of the current cycle count (not defined for TILE-Gx).

unsigned long long get_cycle_count ( void   ) 

Get the current cycle count.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, mpipe/loopback/app.c, tmc/memory_perf/memory.c, tmc/queue/bench_one_to_one.c, and trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.

void cycle_relax ( void   ) 

Delay for a brief period.

As implemented, this function is a five-cycle slow SPR read on tilegx, six cycles on tilepro.
